summary: Moved the 0x8008A698-0x8008A9F8 wait-animation code out of the ftCommon ftCo_08A6 placeholder into melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c, adding typed C implementations for ftCo_8008A698, ftCo_8008A6D8, and ftCo_8008A7A8. The PR reported 93.15% file matching over 864 bytes: ftCo_8008A698 and ftCo_8008A6D8 reached 100%, while ftCo_8008A7A8 reached 90%. It also updated splits/symbols/build configuration for the file relocation and made tools/diff_changes.py more tolerant of missing/null fuzzy-match data.
what_changed: Replaced the tiny ftwaitanim.c string-only stub with real C for ftCo_8008A698, ftCo_8008A6D8, and ftCo_8008A7A8.
Deleted the ftCommon ftCo_08A6.c and ftCo_08A6.h placeholder declarations, which previously used UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and NOT_IMPLEMENTED.
Moved the corresponding assembly fallback from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_08A6.s to asm/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.s, preserving address-based labels for the 0x8008A698-0x8008A9F8 range.
Updated ftCo_Wait.c to include ft/ftwaitanim.h instead of ftCommon/ftCo_08A6.h.
Changed config/GALE01/splits.txt so ftwaitanim.c owns the text range at 0x8008A698-0x8008A9F8 plus its data/sdata/sdata2 ranges, rather than having data split separately and text under ftCo_08A6.c.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to mark ftwaitanim string/float/double constants as local compiler-style @ symbols such as @275, @276, @277, @219, @220, and @222 instead of global/lbl names.
Adjusted configure.py so melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c is placed at the former ftCo_08A6 slot and marked Matching there.
Adjusted obj_files.mk to remove src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c.o from the earlier ft block and replace the old ftCo_08A6 asm object with asm/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.s.o.
Changed two fields in src/melee/ft/types.h from concrete integer types to UNK_T: ftData.x24 and Fighter.x590.
Made tools/diff_changes.py skip null function records and default missing fuzzy_match_percent values to 0 through a get_fuzzy helper.
smart_moves: Grouped the text and data ownership under ftwaitanim.c in splits.txt, which better matches the file's actual string constants and function address range.
Used typed function prototypes in ftwaitanim.h: ftCo_8008A698 takes ftCo_Fighter*, ftCo_8008A6D8 takes ftCo_GObj* plus enum_t anim_id, and ftCo_8008A7A8 takes ftCo_GObj* plus IntVec2*.
Factored the repeated animation-reset sequence into ftCo_8008A6D8 and reused it from ftCo_8008A7A8 rather than duplicating the whole transition logic in C.
Kept a small inline predicate, inlineA0, for the special anim_id values 2 and 31, reflecting a specific branch condition in the larger wait-animation selection routine.
Represented the weighted random wait-animation table as IntVec2 entries, using x as the animation id and y as the weight, with a -1 sentinel.
Preserved the original error path behavior for illegal wait-animation data with OSReport("wait anim data illegal!!\n", max, randi) and HSD_ASSERT(86, 0).
Updated the diff reporting helper to handle incomplete JSON robustly, reducing friction when analyzing partial or imperfect decomp comparison outputs.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder file only exists because code was split incorrectly, moving the functions to the file that owns the data constants can improve both organization and matchability.

For functions that index animation metadata, precise pointer typing matters: this PR used struct S_TEMP4* for fp->x24[anim_id], u8* for blend data at fp->x28 + anim_id * 2, and IntVec2* for weighted id/weight pairs.
A 100% match for small helpers can be reached before the larger caller is fully matched; here ftCo_8008A698 and ftCo_8008A6D8 are reported at 100%, while ftCo_8008A7A8 remains 90%.

Build/split/symbol updates are part of the matching change: moving source ownership without aligning splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, and obj_files.mk risks stale asm paths or incorrect symbol scopes.
Compiler-generated local constants should be reflected as local @ symbols where evidence supports it, rather than forcing global lbl_/ftCo_ names.

Use existing project macros and helpers such as GET_FIGHTER, HSD_ASSERT, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ftAnim_8006EBE8, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftData_80085CD8, and ftCo_8009E7B4 to express known engine behavior.
naming_conventions: Header guard for the relocated wait-animation header changed from GALE01_3C54A8 to GALE01_08A698, aligning the header with the text address range rather than only the data string address.
Unmatched or unknown public functions retained address-based names: ftCo_8008A698, ftCo_8008A6D8, and ftCo_8008A7A8.
Compiler literals in symbols.txt were renamed to local @NNN symbols, including @275/@276 for data strings, @277 for the assert string, and @219/@220/@222 for sdata2 float/double constants.
The deleted ftCommon placeholder name ftCo_08A6 was not kept as the owner once the code moved into ftwaitanim.
The local helper name inlineA0 is provisional/evidence-light; it captures a branch predicate but is not an inferred retail symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The old ftCo_08A6 assembly was deleted and reintroduced as asm/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.s with absolute addresses and file name ftwaitanim.c, matching the corrected split ownership.
The assembly fallback preserved function boundaries for ftCo_8008A698, ftCo_8008A6D8, and ftCo_8008A7A8 while C progress was recorded separately by the PR report.
C code mirrors common PowerPC patterns: early null/sentinel checks, anim_id != -1 guard, x24/x28 indexed pointer arithmetic, and explicit 0.0f/1.0f arguments to recover lfs/lfd constant usage.

The weighted random selection loop uses HSD_Randi(100), adds 1 to create a 1-based threshold, accumulates weights, and repeats if the selected animation equals the current animation except for anim_id 2 or 31.
For byte-to-float conversion behavior, ftCo_8008A6D8 passes *blend_data to ftAnim_8006EBE8 together with 0.0f and 1.0f, matching the assembly's load-byte plus double conversion pattern.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching ftCo_8008A7A8 or rename inlineA0.
Search for later changes to Fighter.x590 and ftData.x24 after this PR changed them to UNK_T.
Look for PRs that replace asm/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.s.o with src/melee/ft/ftwaitanim.c.o in obj_files.mk.
Search for review or later commits discussing whether IntVec2 is the final type for the wait-animation weighted table.
Find other PRs that move text ownership out of ftCommon placeholder files into top-level ft files.
